---
title: The DATA 793 Student GitHub Repository
author: Emily Eames
date: May 5th, 2025
format:
  gfm:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    embed-resources: true
---

# 🌳 Urban Tree Phenology & Climate Sensitivity Analysis - Washington D.C.

## Project Overview
This project analyzes how climate variables and urban environmental factors influence tree phenology in an urban setting. Using regression models and exploratory analysis, the study evaluates how temperature, precipitation, and impervious surface cover affect seasonal timing across multiple tree species. This report will expand Dr. Alonzo’s previous research done in DC and Santa Barbra California.


The goal is to...
1. Analyze satellite NDVI data to understand how species and urban environmental factors influence tree phenology in a semi-arid climate. 

2. Leverage data from NOAA, Worldview, and the local open data.

3.Apply statistical analyses to model—including OLS and phylogenetic generalized least squares—to examine the effects of temperature, precipitation, elevation, species, latitude/longitude, and surface composition on start and end of growing season dates derived from NDVI. 

4. Understand which environmental drivers most strongly influence phenological changes.

---

## Research Question

How do species characteristics (1) , local climate drivers (2) , and urban context (3) influence the timing and duration of tree phenological events (e.g., green-up, peak greenness, senescence) across Washington, DC.



---

## Key Datasets 

- USDA Full Tree Dataset USDA Tree Dataset (project_solution/data)
- NOAA Climate Daily Observation NOAA CDO Dataset (project solutions folder)
- NDVI Outputs NDVI data compilation (project_solution/data)


## Key Outputs

-  Phenology Extraction PhenoMetrics (project_solution/analysis)
-  Final DC Species Output DC Specise List (project_solution/data)
-  rgression Results Table Regression Results Table

## Key Figures

- Pearson Correlation Coefficient [Correlation Plots](final_pcc_heatmap.R)
- Polygon Overlay [Polygon Overlay] (polygonoverlay.R)

##  Repository Structure

├── data/
│ ├── raw/ # Raw datasets
│ └── cleaned/ # Processed datasets
├── scripts/ # R scripts for cleaning + modeling
├── outputs/ # Regression tables and results
├── figs/ # Visualizations (graphs, PCC plots)
├── report/ # Quarto files + final report
└── README.md


---

## Methodology
- Phenology Metric ~ Spring Temperature + Spring Precipitation + Impervious Surface + DBH + Species FE + Year FE
- Model Type: Pooled OLS and Fixed Effects  
- Fixed Effects: Species and Year  
- Interpretation: Coefficients represent changes in phenological timing (in days)

---

## Results Summary

- Higher spring temperatures lead to earlier start of season  
- Precipitation effects can be are weaker and less consistent  
- Urbanization significantly influences phenology  
- Species differ in sensitivity to climate 

---

## Reproducing the Analysis

### Clone the repository
```bash
git clone https://github.com/26S-DATA-793-2/project-eeames00


- The folder contains sub-folders with dummy files in them to support
  typical project work.

- If students are not using this folder, they shall create a README.md
  file to describe how they are configuration managing their project
